petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. No details about the petitioner's field of work, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision text. The petition was filed at the Vermont Service Center under case number EAC 04 130 53554.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was dismissed as moot because USCIS records indicated that the petitioner had already been granted lawful permanent residence on January 10, 2007. Because the petitioner had already obtained the underlying immigration benefit, further pursuit of the matter was unnecessary. The AAO did not reach the merits of whether the petitioner had established sustained national or international acclaim.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: The appeal was dismissed on procedural/mootness grounds rather than on the merits, because the petitioner had already been granted lawful permanent residence on January 10, 2007.
